**Mestre:** <task-notification> <task-id>r5lalautx</task-id> <task-type>remote_agent</task-type> <status>completed</status> <summary>Remote review completed</summary> </task-notification> The remote review produced the following findings: [{"bug_id": "bug_009", "name": "Chunk-error Sentry capture is dead code due to ignoreErrors filter", "severity": "normal", "file_path": "src/components/shared/ErrorBoundary.tsx", "start_line": 65, "end_line": 69, "pr_comment": "The explicit `Sentry.withScope` + `captureException` block for chunk errors in `ErrorBoundary.tsx:65-69` is dead code: every error message it catches is also listed in the `ignoreErrors` array in `src/lib/sentry.ts:101-115` (`/Loading chunk \\d+ failed/`, `/^ChunkLoadError/`, `/Failed to fetch dynamically imported module/`, `/^Load failed$/`), and Sentry's `InboundFilters` integration applies `ignoreErrors` to **both** auto-captured and manually-captured events, dropping them before `beforeSend` runs. So the `errorBoundary: chunk` tag and componentStack context introduced here will never appear in Sentry. Either remove the manual capture (relying on the existing `reportError` \u2192 Supabase path) or drop the chunk-error regexes from `ignoreErrors` \u2014 both pieces were added in this same PR and silently cancel each other out.", "reasoning": "## What the bug is\n\nIn `src/components/shared/ErrorBoundary.tsx:65-69`, the chunk-error branch now does:\n\n```ts\nSentry.withScope((scope) =\u003e {\n  scope.setTag('errorBoundary', 'chunk');\n  scope.setContext('react', { componentStack: errorInfo.componentStack || '' });\n  Sentry.captureException(error);\n});\n```\n\nThe apparent intent is to surface chunk-load failures in Sentry with an `errorBoundary: chunk` tag and componentStack context so the team can distinguish deploy-induced chunk errors from other React errors.\n\nHowever, the same PR also adds an `ignoreErrors` list in `src/lib/sentry.ts:101-115`:\n\n```ts\nignoreErrors: [\n  ...,\n  /^Load failed$/,\n  /Loading chunk \\d+ failed/,\n  /^ChunkLoadError/,\n  /Failed to fetch dynamically imported module/,\n  ...\n],\n```\n\nThese four patterns cover exactly the four substring checks used by the `isChunkError` gate in ErrorBoundary (lines 53-56): `'Loading chunk'`, `'Failed to fetch dynamically imported module'`, `'Load failed'`, `'ChunkLoadError'`.\n\n## Why the manual capture cannot bypass the filter\n\n`ignoreErrors` is implemented by Sentry's built-in `InboundFilters` integration, which registers as a global **event processor**. Event processors run on every event in the SDK pipeline \u2014 irrespective of whether the event originated from automatic instrumentation (global `onerror`, unhandled rejection) or from a manual `Sentry.captureException` / `captureMessage` call. Crucially, this stage runs **before** `beforeSend`. So a `withScope` block that calls `captureException` does not bypass it; the scope's tags and contexts are attached, the event enters the processor pipeline, `InboundFilters` matches the error's message against `ignoreErrors`, and the event is silently dropped.\n\n## Step-by-step proof\n\n1. User on production hits a stale chunk after deploy. Vite throws `TypeError: Failed to fetch dynamically imported module: https://app/assets/Foo-abc.js`.\n2. React's error boundary invokes `componentDidCatch`. `isChunkError` is `true` because the message contains `'Failed to fetch dynamically imported module'`.\n3. `reportError(error, 'ErrorBoundary:chunk', { componentStack })` runs \u2192 Supabase path works fine.\n4. `Sentry.withScope` runs: scope gets `errorBoundary: chunk` tag and `react.componentStack` context.\n5. `Sentry.captureException(error)` enqueues the event into the SDK pipeline.\n6. `InboundFilters` event processor evaluates the event. It checks the exception value (`error.message`) against each entry of `ignoreErrors`. The regex `/Failed to fetch dynamically imported module/` matches.\n7. The processor returns `null` \u2192 event is dropped before `beforeSend` is even called and before transport.\n8. **Result:** zero events for the chunk branch in Sentry. The `errorBoundary: chunk` tag is never visible in the UI.\n\nThe same trace holds for the other three branches:\n- `'Loading chunk X failed'` \u2192 matched by `/Loading chunk \\d+ failed/`\n- `'Load failed'` (Safari fetch-failure message, typically the whole message) \u2192 matched by `/^Load failed$/`\n- `'ChunkLoadError: ...'` or error of type `ChunkLoadError` \u2192 matched by `/^ChunkLoadError/` (`InboundFilters` checks both the message and the exception type)\n\n## Impact\n\nObservability dead code. The team will look at Sentry expecting to see chunk-deploy failures with the `errorBoundary: chunk` tag and componentStack metadata, but will see nothing. They may incorrectly conclude that chunk failures aren't happening, or chase phantom causes for missing reports. The Supabase `reportError` path is unaffected, so this isn't a total observability loss \u2014 but the Sentry-side plumbing introduced by this PR contradicts the `ignoreErrors` list introduced by the same PR.\n\n## How to fix\n\nPick one of:\n\n1. **Remove the manual `Sentry.withScope` / `captureException` block** in the `isChunkError` branch (lines 65-69). Rely on `reportError` for chunk observability. This keeps `ignoreErrors` doing its job of silencing noisy chunk errors in Sentry.\n\n2. **Drop the four chunk-related regexes** (`/Loading chunk \\d+ failed/`, `/^ChunkLoadError/`, `/Failed to fetch dynamically imported module/`, `/^Load failed$/`) from `ignoreErrors` in `src/lib/sentry.ts`. The manual capture then takes effect and the team gets the tagged events.\n\n3. **Use `Sentry.captureMessage` with a distinct fingerprint** and gate it through `beforeSend` with an allowlist (e.g. only allow events whose tag is `errorBoundary: chunk`). More complex than (1) or (2) but lets chunk noise stay suppressed globally while still surfacing the explicit captures.", "corroborated_by": null, "source_bug_ids": ["bug_009"], "created_at": 1780019564.2418966, "_file_path": "/tmp/bughunter_work/reports/bug_009_report.json"}, {"bug_id": "bug_014", "name": "user_role tag stays stale when role transitions to null mid-session", "severity": "nit", "file_path": "src/hooks/useAuth.tsx", "start_line": 25, "end_line": 25, "pr_comment": "In `syncSentryUser` (src/hooks/useAuth.tsx:25), the `if (role) Sentry.setTag(\"user_role\", role)` guard prevents the tag from being cleared when a logged-in user transitions to a null role mid-session \u2014 e.g. on a re-login after admin-side role revocation, where `fetchProfileAndRole` returns no recognized roles and `getPrimaryRole` returns `null`. The previously set `user_role` value (e.g. `\"admin\"`) remains attached to the Sentry scope and mislabels subsequent events. Fix: drop the guard and call `Sentry.setTag(\"user_role\", role)` unconditionally (passing null clears it), or use `Sentry.getCurrentScope().setTag(\"user_role\", role ?? undefined)`.", "reasoning": "**What the bug is**\n\nInside `syncSentryUser`, the logged-in branch sets the `user_role` tag only when `role` is truthy:\n\n```ts\nSentry.setUser({ id: user.id, ...(fullName ? { username: fullName } : {}) });\nif (role) Sentry.setTag('user_role', role);\n```\n\nBecause the call is gated, a transition from a non-null role (`'admin'` / `'teacher'` / `'director'`) to `null` cannot overwrite or clear the previously set tag \u2014 the stale value remains attached to the scope and is emitted on every subsequent Sentry event until the next logout-driven `Sentry.setUser(null)` resets it.\n\n**The code path that triggers it**\n\n`fetchProfileAndRole` resolves `setRole(getPrimaryRole(fetchedRoles))`, and `getPrimaryRole` (src/lib/authRouting.ts:5-23) returns `null` whenever the filtered role array contains no `student`/`teacher`/`director` entries. So a successful fetch that returns an empty `user_roles` row \u2014 admin revoked the role, the row was deleted, or only unrecognized values came back \u2014 produces `setRole(null)` while `user` is still the logged-in user. The `useEffect` on lines 69-71 re-runs `syncSentryUser(user, null, fullName)`, hits the `if (role)` guard, and silently skips `setTag`. The prior `'admin'` value is still pinned to the current scope.\n\n**Concrete trigger scenario (step-by-step proof)**\n\n1. User A signs in as admin. `fetchProfileAndRole` returns `['admin']`, `setRole('admin')` fires, `useEffect` runs `syncSentryUser(A, 'admin', name)` \u2192 `Sentry.setTag('user_role', 'admin')`. Scope tag = `admin`.\n2. Backend admin revokes A's role (deletes the row in `user_roles`). A's session is still valid.\n3. A signs out via the in-app button, then signs back in (very common). `signIn` does `fetchingForRef.current = null`, then `onAuthStateChange(SIGNED_IN)` fires, `setUser(A)` runs, `fetchProfileAndRole(A.id)` is invoked again \u2014 this time `fetchedRoles = []` and `getPrimaryRole([]) === null`, so `setRole(null)`.\n4. The state-sync `useEffect` re-runs with `(user=A, role=null)`. The `!user` branch is skipped (user is non-null), and the `if (role)` guard at line 25 makes `setTag` a no-op.\n5. The Sentry scope still carries `tags.user_role = 'admin'` from step 1 (or whatever was last successfully set). Every event A produces from this point on \u2014 UI errors, captureException calls \u2014 is mislabeled `user_role: admin` even though A is no longer an admin.\n\n**Why existing code doesn't prevent it**\n\nThe `catch` block in `fetchProfileAndRole` only logs and does not reset roles \u2014 that's correct behavior. But the *success* path with an empty result set still produces `setRole(null)` without a corresponding `setUser(null)`. None of the three other `setRole(null)` sites help here because they're paired with `setUser(null)` (and so hit the `!user` branch that does clear the tag). The logged-in branch is the only place this matters and it's the one that's guarded.\n\n**Impact**\n\nObservability data quality, not user-facing functionality. The `user_role` tag is used for filtering and grouping Sentry events; stale values will misattribute errors to the wrong cohort during triage. No security or correctness impact on the app itself.\n\n**Addressing the refutation**\n\nOne reviewer argued the bug cannot manifest because every `setRole(null)` is paired with `setUser(null)`. This is true for the three explicit sites (`clearAuthState`, the signed-out branch in `onAuthStateChange`, and the `signOut` method), but it misses the implicit fourth site: `fetchProfileAndRole`'s success path, which calls `setRole(getPrimaryRole(fetchedRoles))` without touching `user`. When `fetchedRoles` is empty (genuinely no roles, or all values filtered out by `.filter(Boolean)` then by `getPrimaryRole`'s whitelist), `setRole(null)` fires while `user` remains the logged-in user. Re-login after role revocation is the most common trigger; multi-tab `SIGNED_IN` re-emission for a different user can also produce a brief window with the same shape.\n\n**Fix**\n\nDrop the guard so `null` overwrites:\n```ts\nSentry.setTag('user_role', role);\n```\nOr, to actually remove the key rather than set it to `null` (which Sentry serializes as `tags.user_role: null` in some SDK paths):\n```ts\nSentry.getCurrentScope().setTag('user_role', role ?? undefined);\n```", "corroborated_by": null, "source_bug_ids": ["bug_014"], "created_at": 1780019623.646388, "_file_path": "/tmp/bughunter_work/reports/bug_014_report.json"}, {"bug_id": "bug_013", "name": "Session Replay leaks Supabase auth tokens from password-reset URL hash", "severity": "normal", "file_path": "src/lib/sentry.ts", "start_line": 90, "end_line": 91, "pr_comment": "Session Replay is enabled in production (`replaysOnErrorSampleRate: 1.0`) with no URL-level redaction, so rrweb captures `location.href` \u2014 including the `#access_token=...&refresh_token=...` hash that Supabase recovery deep links land with on `/reset-password`. `maskAllText`/`maskAllInputs` only mask DOM content; `beforeSend`/`sanitizeEvent` only runs on error events and keys on field names, never replay URL frames. Any error during the recovery flow uploads a replay containing a live Supabase session token, letting a Sentry-project viewer impersonate the user \u2014 directly contradicting `sendDefaultPii: false`. Fix: pass `beforeAddRecordingEvent` to `replayIntegration` to strip hash/query params (or skip replay on `/reset-password` and `/~oauth` entirely), and add `access_token`/`refresh_token`/`token_hash`/`code` to `SENSITIVE_KEYWORDS`.", "reasoning": "## What the bug is\n\nThis PR enables Sentry Session Replay in production (`src/lib/sentry.ts:90-91`):\n```\nreplaysSessionSampleRate: isProduction ? 0.05 : 0,\nreplaysOnErrorSampleRate: isProduction ? 1.0 : 0,\n```\nThe `replayIntegration` is configured with `maskAllText: true` and `maskAllInputs: true`, but those options only mask DOM **content**. They do not redact URLs. Sentry's underlying rrweb recorder captures `document.URL` / `location.href` (including the hash and query string) in navigation / Meta events, and there is no `beforeAddRecordingEvent`, no `mutateUrl`, no `networkDetailDenyUrls`, and no per-route guard on the integration.\n\n## How the leak path triggers\n\nSupabase password-recovery deep links land at:\n```\nhttps://app/reset-password#access_token=\u003cjwt\u003e&refresh_token=\u003crt\u003e&type=recovery\n```\n`src/pages/ResetPasswordPage.tsx:30-41` confirms the page reads these from `window.location.hash` directly:\n```\nhashParams = new URLSearchParams(window.location.hash.replace(/^#/, ''));\n...\nhasAccessToken: hashParams.has('access_token'),\naccessToken: hashParams.get('access_token'),\nrefreshToken: hashParams.get('refresh_token'),\n```\n`src/main.tsx:10` calls `initSentry()` at module top-level **before** `createRoot(...).render(\u003cApp /\u003e)` on line 25, so the recorder is alive from the very first paint \u2014 it captures the recovery URL (with the secrets in the fragment) in its initial Meta event before the page can do anything about it.\n\n## Why existing redaction does not save us\n\n- `beforeSend` only runs for **error** events, not replay events. Replay envelopes are uploaded on a separate transport and never pass through it.\n- `sanitizeEvent` / `redactSensitiveFields` walks **object keys** and replaces values whose key matches `SENSITIVE_KEYWORDS`. URL strings inside replay frames are opaque payloads, not keyed fields.\n- `SENSITIVE_KEYWORDS` (`src/lib/sentry.ts:5-19`) does not include `access_token`, `refresh_token`, `token_hash`, or `code` anyway \u2014 so even if it could reach replay URLs, it would miss them.\n\n## Step-by-step proof\n\n1. User receives a password-recovery email. Link is `https://app/reset-password#access_token=eyJhbGc...&refresh_token=...&type=recovery`.\n2. Browser navigates to the URL. Vite loads `main.tsx`, which executes `initSentry()` on line 10 \u2014 `Sentry.init` starts the Replay recorder. rrweb emits a Meta event that includes `location.href` verbatim, including the hash.\n3. React renders `\u003cApp /\u003e`; `ResetPasswordPage` mounts and reads the tokens from `window.location.hash` (lines 30-41).\n4. The recovery flow exercises `supabase.auth.setSession`, `verifyOtp`, `exchangeCodeForSession`, plus a `checkPasswordLeak` HaveIBeenPwned call and `supabase.auth.updateUser`. Any of these can throw (network blip, expired OTP, validation error, password-leak fetch failure, etc.).\n5. The thrown error is captured by Sentry. Because `replaysOnErrorSampleRate: 1.0`, the buffered replay (containing the Meta event with the original URL) is flushed and uploaded.\n6. Anyone with read access to the Sentry project \u2014 viewers, support staff, security investigators \u2014 opens the replay timeline. The very first event lists the URL including `#access_token=...&refresh_token=...`. These tokens are valid Supabase credentials for the access_token TTL (default ~1h, refresh_token longer).\n7. They paste the tokens into `supabase.auth.setSession(...)` from any client and have a fully authenticated session as the affected user. Account takeover.\n\nThe `/~oauth` callback (referenced in `vite.config.ts` `navigateFallbackDenylist`) and `/create-password` (which also reads `token_hash` from the hash) have the same shape.\n\n## Impact\n\nThis is a security regression introduced by this PR: prior to it, both replay sample rates were 0, so the leak channel didn't exist. Now anyone who triggers an error during password recovery \u2014 and the recovery flow is *full* of throw-able paths \u2014 has their auth tokens uploaded to Sentry where every Sentry-project viewer can read them. This directly contradicts the explicit `sendDefaultPii: false` and the careful `sanitizeEvent` redaction layer.\n\n## How to fix\n\nAny of the following, ideally combined:\n- Pass `beforeAddRecordingEvent` to `replayIntegration` that strips `location.hash` and known-sensitive query params (`access_token`, `refresh_token`, `token_hash`, `code`, `type=recovery`) from any URL field in the event.\n- Use Sentry's `networkDetailDenyUrls` / `mutateUrl` helpers to redact the same params from network breadcrumbs.\n- Skip replay initialization (or call `replay.stop()`) on token-bearing routes: `/reset-password`, `/create-password`, `/~oauth`.\n- Add `access_token` / `refresh_token` / `token_hash` / `code` to `SENSITIVE_KEYWORDS` as a defense-in-depth layer (won't help replays alone but reduces other risk).", "corroborated_by": null, "source_bug_ids": ["bug_013"], "created_at": 1780019573.3760276, "_file_path": "/tmp/bughunter_work/reports/bug_013_report.json"}, {"bug_id": "bug_003", "name": "denyUrls regex /extensions?\\// is overbroad and matches legitimate paths", "severity": "nit", "file_path": "src/lib/sentry.ts", "start_line": 117, "end_line": 117, "pr_comment": "The first entry in `denyUrls`, `/extensions?\\//i`, is unanchored and case-insensitive \u2014 it matches the substring `extension/` or `extensions/` anywhere in a stack-frame URL, so any third-party script with that path segment (e.g. `https://cdn.example.com/v1/extensions/foo.js`) would be silently dropped from Sentry. The three regexes that follow already cover the intended browser-extension schemes (`chrome-extension://`, `moz-extension://`, `safari-extension://`) \u2014 and those scheme URLs do not even match this broad pattern (they have `extension:` not `extension/`), so it isn't a useful fallback either. Recommend deleting the line, or anchoring it to a scheme like `/-extension:\\\\/\\\\//i`.", "reasoning": "### The pattern\n\n```ts\ndenyUrls: [\n  /extensions?\\//i,           // \u003c-- this one\n  /^chrome:\\/\\//i,\n  /^chrome-extension:\\/\\//i,\n  /^moz-extension:\\/\\//i,\n  /^safari-extension:\\/\\//i,\n],\n```\n\nSentry matches `denyUrls` against the URL where each stack frame originates (i.e. the script file URL). The first regex is **unanchored on both sides** and **case-insensitive**, so it matches any URL containing the substring `extension/` or `extensions/` \u2014 at any position in the path.\n\n### Why the broad regex is not a useful fallback\n\nThe three regexes immediately below it already anchor on `^chrome-extension://`, `^moz-extension://`, and `^safari-extension://`, which precisely target the URL schemes browsers use to load extension content. Notice these scheme URLs **do not match** `/extensions?\\//i` \u2014 `chrome-extension://abc/x.js` contains `extension:` (colon) and `extension://` (colon-slash-slash), never the bare substring `extension/` that the broad pattern looks for. So the broad regex is not even a redundant safety net for the intended target \u2014 it catches a different, and likely unintended, class of URLs.\n\n### Step-by-step proof of a false positive\n\nImagine a future error originating from a third-party script bundled into the page:\n\n1. App loads a vendor chunk hosted at `https://cdn.example.com/v1/extensions/widget.js`.\n2. `widget.js` throws a TypeError; Sentry captures the stack frame with that file URL.\n3. Sentry walks `denyUrls` and tests `/extensions?\\//i` against `https://cdn.example.com/v1/extensions/widget.js`.\n4. The substring `extensions/` matches at index 25 \u2192 the event is silently dropped.\n\nThe same happens for any URL with `/extension/` or `/extensions/` in the path: e.g. `https://assets.foo.io/admin/extension/main.js`, or a future app route under `/api/extensions/...`.\n\n### Impact\n\nIn this repo today there are no routes or vendor bundles containing `/extension/`, so the practical blast radius is low \u2014 which is why I'm filing this as a **nit** rather than a normal bug. But the tradeoff is asymmetric: zero upside (since the scheme regexes already cover real extensions), with a silent error-suppression downside if any third-party script with that path is ever added. Silent suppression of Sentry events is exactly the kind of thing that's expensive to notice later, because you don't see what you're missing.\n\n### Fix\n\nEither delete the line entirely (the three scheme regexes are sufficient), or anchor it to a scheme such as:\n\n```ts\n/-extension:\\/\\//i\n```\n\n\u2026which is a single pattern that subsumes `chrome-extension://`, `moz-extension://`, and `safari-extension://` without matching arbitrary paths.", "corroborated_by": null, "source_bug_ids": ["bug_003"], "created_at": 1780019553.6931195, "_file_path": "/tmp/bughunter_work/reports/bug_003_report.json"}, {"bug_id": "bug_002", "name": "Session Replay records essay images, bypassing PII redaction", "severity": "normal", "file_path": "src/lib/sentry.ts", "start_line": 96, "end_line": 99, "pr_comment": "Session Replay is enabled with `blockAllMedia: false` and only `[data-private]`/`.sentry-block` in the block list, while essay images are rendered as bare `\u003cimg src={...}\u003e` (e.g. src/components/teacher/EssayCorrection.tsx:122, src/components/student/EssayWriterView.tsx:192,331) with neither attribute. Combined with `replaysOnErrorSampleRate: 1.0` in production, any error during essay grading/submission ships a replay containing the verbatim student-essay photo to Sentry \u2014 bypassing the `SENSITIVE_KEYWORDS` redaction this same file sets up for 'essay'/'redacao'/'reda\u00e7\u00e3o'. Fix: set `blockAllMedia: true`, or add an essay-specific selector to the `block` array and tag the relevant `\u003cimg\u003e` accordingly.", "reasoning": "## What is wrong\n\n`src/lib/sentry.ts` lines 5-18 declare `'essay'`, `'redacao'`, `'reda\u00e7\u00e3o'` as `SENSITIVE_KEYWORDS` and the `beforeSend`/`sanitizeEvent` pipeline redacts any matching key in `request`/`user`/`extra`/`contexts`. The codebase is explicitly stating that essay content is PII.\n\nThis PR turns Session Replay on in production for the first time with this config (lines 90-99):\n\n```ts\nreplaysSessionSampleRate: isProduction ? 0.05 : 0,\nreplaysOnErrorSampleRate: isProduction ? 1.0 : 0,\nintegrations: [\n  Sentry.replayIntegration({\n    maskAllText: true,\n    maskAllInputs: true,\n    blockAllMedia: false,\n    block: ['[data-private]', '.sentry-block'],\n    mask: ['[data-mask]', '.sentry-mask'],\n  }),\n],\n```\n\n`maskAllText` and `maskAllInputs` only scrub text nodes and form-input values; they do **not** cover `\u003cimg\u003e`, `\u003ccanvas\u003e`, `\u003cvideo\u003e`, or `\u003caudio\u003e`. The dedicated switch for media is `blockAllMedia`, and it is set to `false`. The `block` allowlist only contains `[data-private]` and `.sentry-block`.\n\n## Step-by-step proof\n\n1. A student writes/uploads a handwritten essay. In `src/components/student/EssayWriterView.tsx:181` and `:326` they pick an image file; the preview renders as `\u003cimg src={imagePreview} alt=\"Preview\" \u2026\u003e` at lines 192 and 331 \u2014 no `data-private` attribute, no `sentry-block` class.\n2. A teacher opens the same essay for correction. In `src/components/teacher/EssayCorrection.tsx:122` the upload is rendered as `\u003cimg src={selectedEssay.imageUrl} alt=\"Reda\u00e7\u00e3o\" className=\"max-w-full rounded-lg mb-4 border\" /\u003e` \u2014 again, no matching attribute/class.\n3. Any unhandled React error or rejected fetch fires while one of those views is mounted (the codebase routes errors through `ErrorBoundary` and reports them to Sentry \u2014 `src/components/shared/ErrorBoundary.tsx` was just wired up in this PR with `Sentry.captureException`).\n4. With `replaysOnErrorSampleRate: 1.0`, the replay buffer for that session is uploaded to Sentry. rrweb's snapshot of the DOM includes the rendered `\u003cimg\u003e` bytes verbatim because `blockAllMedia` is `false` and no selector matches.\n5. The `beforeSend` sanitizer runs on the **error event** payload only \u2014 it cannot touch replay frames, which are uploaded over the separate replay channel. The `SENSITIVE_KEYWORDS`/`redactSensitiveFields` machinery is bypassed.\n\nNet effect: a Sentry project viewer can scrub through the replay and see the handwritten essay photo \u2014 exactly the PII this file's own `SENSITIVE_KEYWORDS` list says must be redacted.\n\n## Why existing protections do not help\n\n- **maskAllText / maskAllInputs**: text-only; not media.\n- **block: ['[data-private]', '.sentry-block']**: the essay `\u003cimg\u003e` elements have neither.\n- **beforeSend / sanitizeEvent / redactSensitiveFields**: runs on the error event, not on replay frames.\n- **sendDefaultPii: false**: controls whether Sentry sets request headers/IP automatically; has no effect on replay media.\n\n## Impact\n\n`replaysOnErrorSampleRate: 1.0` means *every* production error in a session that has displayed an essay image will upload a video containing that image. Given how often React render errors, network errors, or chunk-load errors fire, this is a high-traffic leak channel for the most sensitive data type the app handles (and the only image-shaped PII).\n\n## Fix\n\nEasiest: set `blockAllMedia: true` on `Sentry.replayIntegration({...})`. This blocks all media by default and matches the privacy posture documented in `SENSITIVE_KEYWORDS`.\n\nIf product needs other media captured for debugging, add a per-image block selector and tag the essay images:\n\n```ts\nSentry.replayIntegration({\n  \u2026,\n  block: ['[data-private]', '.sentry-block', '.essay-image', '[data-essay]'],\n});\n```\n\n\u2026and add `className=\"essay-image\"` (or `data-essay`) to the `\u003cimg\u003e` elements in `EssayCorrection.tsx:122`, `EssayWriterView.tsx:192`, `EssayWriterView.tsx:331`, and any other essay-image render site.", "corroborated_by": null, "source_bug_ids": ["bug_002"], "created_at": 1780019568.5652254, "_file_path": "/tmp/bughunter_work/reports/bug_002_report.json"}, {"bug_id": "bug_001", "name": "Source maps shipped publicly when SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN absent", "severity": "normal", "file_path": "vite.config.ts", "start_line": 89, "end_line": 90, "pr_comment": "**Source maps leak to production when `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN` is absent.** `vite.config.ts` sets `build.sourcemap: true` unconditionally (line 90), but the only cleanup \u2014 `filesToDeleteAfterUpload: [\"./dist/**/*.map\"]` \u2014 lives inside the Sentry plugin block, which short-circuits to `false` when `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN` is missing (Vercel preview deploys, fork PR builds, local `npm run build`, CI without the secret). In those cases the deployed `dist/` ships both the `.map` files **and** the `//# sourceMappingURL=` comments inlined in every JS bundle, letting any visitor reconstruct the original TypeScript/JSX source. Fix: use `sourcemap: \"hidden\"` (Sentry still consumes the maps; the URL comment is omitted so leaked files aren't advertised) or gate `build.sourcemap` on the same `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN` check.", "reasoning": "### What the bug is\n\n`vite.config.ts` line 90 sets `build.sourcemap: true` unconditionally. Vite's default behavior for `sourcemap: true` is twofold:\n\n1. Emit a `.map` file alongside every JS chunk in `dist/`.\n2. Inline a `//# sourceMappingURL=\u003cchunk\u003e.js.map` comment at the end of every JS bundle.\n\nThe only mechanism that removes those `.map` files is `filesToDeleteAfterUpload: [\"./dist/**/*.map\"]` inside the `sentryVitePlugin` block (vite.config.ts:65-82). That entire plugin entry is gated on:\n\n```js\nmode === \"production\" &&\n  process.env.SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN &&\n  sentryVitePlugin({ ... })\n```\n\nIf either condition is falsy, the whole subexpression resolves to `false` and the trailing `.filter(Boolean)` strips it from the plugins array. No warning, no fallback cleanup \u2014 sourcemap generation stays on, deletion goes away.\n\n### The leak scenarios\n\nThere are several realistic paths where `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN` is absent during a production build:\n\n- **Vercel preview deploys** \u2014 Vercel scopes environment variables per environment by default. Teams commonly mark Sentry secrets as Production-only, so preview deployments build with `mode === \"production\"` but without the token. The preview URL then publicly serves `.map` files.\n- **Fork PR builds** \u2014 CI workflows triggered from forks don't receive repository secrets, so any production build in that context emits and ships maps.\n- **Local production builds** \u2014 A developer running `npm run build` to test prod assets locally (or to upload a static export somewhere) will produce a `dist/` with maps and sourceMappingURL comments still intact.\n- **CI without the secret** \u2014 A new runner, a rotated/expired token, or a forgotten secret configuration all result in the same silent failure mode.\n\n### Step-by-step proof\n\n1. Run `npm run build` without setting `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN`.\n2. The plugins array evaluates the Sentry entry as `\"production\" && undefined && sentryVitePlugin(...)` \u2192 `undefined`. `.filter(Boolean)` removes it.\n3. Vite/Rollup proceeds with `build.sourcemap: true` (line 90), emitting `dist/assets/index-\u003chash\u003e.js` and `dist/assets/index-\u003chash\u003e.js.map`.\n4. Each JS bundle ends with `//# sourceMappingURL=index-\u003chash\u003e.js.map`.\n5. `dist/` is uploaded to the host (Vercel preview, static hosting, etc.).\n6. An attacker visits the site, opens DevTools (or runs `curl`), grabs the `sourceMappingURL` from any `.js` file, fetches the corresponding `.map`, and reconstructs the original TypeScript/JSX source.\n\n### Why existing code doesn't prevent it\n\nThe team's intent is clear from `filesToDeleteAfterUpload` \u2014 original source is not for publication. But the cleanup step lives inside the very plugin that requires the auth token to run. There is no unconditional sweep, no `sourcemap: \"hidden\"` setting (which would at least omit the URL comment), and no environment-aware gate on the `build.sourcemap` flag itself.\n\n### Impact\n\nThis is a source/IP disclosure regression introduced by this PR. The previous `vite.config.ts` had no `sourcemap` key, defaulting to `false`. The diff at line +90 (`sourcemap: true`) is what makes the exposure possible. Severity is normal \u2014 not a crash or auth bypass, but a real-world risk that any preview deploy or fork build leaks the codebase publicly.\n\n### Suggested fix\n\nAny of the following are sufficient:\n\n```js\n// Option A \u2014 recommended: hidden sourcemaps\nbuild: {\n  sourcemap: \"hidden\",  // Sentry still consumes the map; no URL comment in bundles\n  ...\n}\n\n// Option B \u2014 gate sourcemap on the same condition as upload\nbuild: {\n  sourcemap: mode === \"production\" && !!process.env.SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN,\n  ...\n}\n\n// Option C \u2014 always run cleanup, even without Sentry\n// (add a small plugin that deletes dist/**/*.map in closeBundle unconditionally)\n```\n\nOption A is the Sentry-recommended pattern: maps are still uploaded for symbolication, but a leaked `.map` file is no longer advertised by every JS bundle, dropping the discoverability to near-zero.", "corroborated_by": null, "source_bug_ids": ["bug_001"], "created_at": 1780019562.2483606, "_file_path": "/tmp/bughunter_work/reports/bug_001_report.json"}]

**Mestre:** <task-notification> <task-id>r9zyqtj1e</task-id> <task-type>remote_agent</task-type> <status>completed</status> <summary>Remote review completed</summary> </task-notification> The remote review produced the following findings: [{"bug_id": "bug_006", "name": "Chunk-error Sentry capture is dead code \u2014 ignoreErrors drops every event", "severity": "normal", "file_path": "src/components/shared/ErrorBoundary.tsx", "start_line": 65, "end_line": 69, "pr_comment": "The chunk-error branch in `componentDidCatch` (lines 65-69) explicitly captures the exception to Sentry with an `errorBoundary: 'chunk'` tag and componentStack context, but the new `ignoreErrors` list in `sentry.ts` includes `/Loading chunk \\d+ failed/`, `/^ChunkLoadError/`, `/Failed to fetch dynamically imported module/`, and `/^Load failed$/` \u2014 the exact message shapes `isChunkError` detects. Sentry's InboundFilters runs against the captured exception message regardless of scope/tags, so every event from this branch is dropped before transport, leaving the tag/context as dead code. Either remove the chunk patterns from `ignoreErrors` (if you want this telemetry) or drop this `Sentry.captureException` block (if chunk noise is intentionally suppressed and `reportError` to Supabase is enough).", "reasoning": "**What's happening**\n\nTwo pieces of this PR contradict each other:\n\n1. `src/components/shared/ErrorBoundary.tsx` (lines 52-71) detects chunk errors by matching the error message against `'Loading chunk'`, `'Failed to fetch dynamically imported module'`, `'Load failed'`, and `'ChunkLoadError'`. When matched, it explicitly calls:\n   ```ts\n   Sentry.withScope((scope) =\u003e {\n     scope.setTag('errorBoundary', 'chunk');\n     scope.setContext('react', { componentStack: errorInfo.componentStack || '' });\n     Sentry.captureException(error);\n   });\n   ```\n   The clear intent is to ship these errors to Sentry *with* the boundary tag and React context attached.\n\n2. `src/lib/sentry.ts` (lines 100-113) \u2014 added in the same PR \u2014 configures `ignoreErrors` with patterns that mirror exactly those four message shapes:\n   ```ts\n   /^Load failed$/,\n   /Loading chunk \\d+ failed/,\n   /^ChunkLoadError/,\n   /Failed to fetch dynamically imported module/,\n   ```\n\n**Why the captureException is dead code**\n\nSentry's `ignoreErrors` option is implemented by the `InboundFilters` default integration. It runs in the event-processing pipeline against the captured exception's `type` + `message` (substring for strings, `.test()` for regex), and drops matching events *before transport*. Critically, `withScope` only attaches tags/context to the scope \u2014 it does **not** bypass `InboundFilters`. From the Sentry docs / SDK behavior: `ignoreErrors` applies to every event regardless of how it was produced (auto-instrumented unhandled errors or manual `captureException`).\n\nSo every realistic chunk error that hits the chunk branch \u2014 Webpack/Vite's `'Loading chunk N failed'`, Chrome's `'ChunkLoadError: ...'`, Vite's `'Failed to fetch dynamically imported module: ...'`, Safari's `'Load failed'` \u2014 matches a corresponding `ignoreErrors` pattern and is silently dropped. The `errorBoundary: 'chunk'` tag and componentStack context never reach Sentry's backend.\n\n**Step-by-step proof**\n\n1. A user visits the app after a deploy that invalidated their cached chunk hashes.\n2. Routing triggers a lazy import; Vite throws `TypeError: Failed to fetch dynamically imported module: https://.../assets/Home-abc123.js`.\n3. The error propagates to `ErrorBoundary.componentDidCatch`.\n4. `isChunkError` is `true` (message contains `'Failed to fetch dynamically imported module'`) \u2192 enters the chunk branch.\n5. `reportError(error, 'ErrorBoundary:chunk', ...)` posts to Supabase \u2014 fine.\n6. `Sentry.withScope(...)` runs; `scope.setTag('errorBoundary', 'chunk')` and `setContext('react', ...)` mutate the active scope.\n7. `Sentry.captureException(error)` creates an event with `exception.values[0].value = 'Failed to fetch dynamically imported module: ...'` and the tag/context.\n8. The event enters `InboundFilters`. The regex `/Failed to fetch dynamically imported module/` tests against the exception message \u2192 matches.\n9. The event is dropped. **Zero network call to Sentry.** The tag, context, and entire `withScope` block produced no observable Sentry output.\n\nThe same proof applies for `Loading chunk N failed` (matched by `/Loading chunk \\d+ failed/`), `ChunkLoadError: ...` (matched by `/^ChunkLoadError/`), and `Load failed` (matched by `/^Load failed$/`).\n\n**Why existing code doesn't prevent it**\n\nThere is no `beforeSend` rewrite of the message and no conditional bypass for tagged events. The only output channels for chunk errors are `reportError` \u2192 Supabase (which still works) and the now-dead `Sentry.captureException`. Since the same PR introduces both halves of the contradiction, it's a fresh inconsistency, not pre-existing.\n\n**Impact**\n\nEither the chunk-branch Sentry call was added expecting visibility (in which case observability is silently lost), or chunk noise was meant to be filtered out (in which case the captureException block is misleading dead code that a future reader will assume is functional). Practically, the team loses the ability to see chunk errors *with the ErrorBoundary metadata* in Sentry \u2014 they'll see only Supabase entries via `reportError`. Severity sits between \"nit\" and \"normal\" \u2014 there's no user-facing harm, but the diff explicitly tries to instrument chunk errors and the instrumentation produces nothing.\n\n**Fix**\n\nPick one of:\n\n1. **Keep ErrorBoundary capture, drop the chunk patterns from `ignoreErrors`** \u2014 chunk errors flow to Sentry with the `errorBoundary='chunk'` tag and componentStack. The PR comment in sentry.ts can keep the React-internal / extension / ResizeObserver filters.\n2. **Keep `ignoreErrors`, delete lines 65-69 in ErrorBoundary.tsx** \u2014 chunk errors are noise; `reportError` to Supabase is sufficient, and the dead Sentry call is removed so it doesn't mislead future readers.\n3. **Rewrite the exception in `beforeSend`** when the `errorBoundary` tag is set, so the InboundFilters check sees a non-matching message \u2014 more complexity for marginal benefit; option 1 or 2 is cleaner.", "corroborated_by": null, "source_bug_ids": ["bug_006"], "created_at": 1780028213.332687, "_file_path": "/tmp/bughunter_work/reports/bug_006_report.json"}, {"bug_id": "bug_005", "name": "Source maps shipped to production when SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN is missing", "severity": "normal", "file_path": "vite.config.ts", "start_line": 65, "end_line": 82, "pr_comment": "**Source maps shipped to production when `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN` is missing.** `build.sourcemap: true` (vite.config.ts:90) unconditionally emits `.js.map` files for every production build, but the only thing that deletes them \u2014 `filesToDeleteAfterUpload` inside `sentryVitePlugin` \u2014 is gated on `mode === \"production\" && process.env.SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN` (line 65-66). If that env var is unset/empty/rotated/missing on a preview deploy, the plugin is `.filter(Boolean)`-ed out, Vite still emits the maps, and they get served publicly from `/assets/*.js.map`, leaking readable source (auth flow, supabase calls, role logic, sanitization keywords). Fix: gate `sourcemap` on the same condition (e.g. `sourcemap: !!process.env.SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN && \"hidden\"`), or always delete `dist/**/*.map` in a post-build step.", "reasoning": "### What the bug is\n\nThe diff makes two coupled changes in `vite.config.ts`:\n\n1. Adds `build.sourcemap: true` (line 90), which tells Rollup to emit `.js.map` files alongside every chunk into `dist/assets/`.\n2. Adds a conditionally-included `sentryVitePlugin(...)` entry to the `plugins` array (lines 65-82), gated on `mode === \"production\" && process.env.SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN`. The plugin carries `filesToDeleteAfterUpload: [\"./dist/**/*.map\"]`, which is the ONLY mechanism in the repo that deletes the source maps after the build.\n\nThese two changes have different activation conditions. Source-map generation is unconditional; source-map deletion requires `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN` to be set. When the token is missing for any reason, maps are generated but never deleted \u2014 and Vercel will happily serve them as static assets.\n\n### How it manifests\n\nConcrete proof, step by step, assuming a Vercel deploy where `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN` is unset, empty, or its secret reference has been broken:\n\n1. CI runs `vite build`. `mode` resolves to `\"production\"`.\n2. The plugins array is constructed. For the Sentry plugin entry, JS evaluates `mode === \"production\" && process.env.SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN && sentryVitePlugin(...)`. With the token missing, `process.env.SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN` is `undefined` (or `\"\"`), the short-circuit returns that falsy value, and `sentryVitePlugin(...)` is never even called.\n3. `.filter(Boolean)` strips the falsy entry. The final plugins array contains React, PWA, image optimizer \u2014 but no Sentry plugin.\n4. The `build` block is still active and includes `sourcemap: true`. Rollup emits `dist/assets/index-\u003chash\u003e.js` AND `dist/assets/index-\u003chash\u003e.js.map` for every chunk.\n5. No `filesToDeleteAfterUpload` hook ever fires (the plugin was never registered).\n6. Vercel uploads the entire `dist/` directory. `vercel.json` only contains SPA rewrites that explicitly exclude `/assets/`, so `.map` files in `dist/assets/` are served as static assets with no rewrite.\n7. An attacker fetches `https://\u003csite\u003e/assets/index-\u003chash\u003e.js.map` and reconstructs readable source.\n\n### Why existing code doesn't prevent it\n\nI checked for compensating mechanisms and there are none in this diff or the surrounding repo:\n\n- `.gitignore` only adds `.vercel` (CLI metadata), not deployment filtering.\n- `vercel.json` doesn't exclude `.map` files from serving \u2014 only SPA fallback rewrites.\n- There is no `.vercelignore`.\n- No post-build script in `package.json` deletes the maps.\n- The Sentry plugin's `filesToDeleteAfterUpload` only runs as part of the plugin's lifecycle, which is skipped entirely when the plugin is filtered out.\n\nSo a single missing env var is sufficient to leak source code.\n\n### Impact\n\nThe leaked source maps expose:\n- Full readable auth flow in `useAuth.tsx` (Supabase calls, role logic, session validation patterns).\n- The sanitization keyword list in `src/lib/sentry.ts` (telling attackers which fields are considered sensitive, e.g. \"cpf\", \"cartao\", \"redacao\").\n- Internal route maps, lazy-loaded chunk names, business logic, and any build-time inlined non-secret env vars (anything prefixed with `VITE_`).\n\nThis is a regression introduced by this PR \u2014 `build.sourcemap` was not set to `true` before.\n\n### How to fix\n\nThe cleanest fix is to couple the two changes so source-maps only exist when something will delete them. Replace:\n\n```ts\nbuild: {\n  sourcemap: true,\n  ...\n}\n```\n\nwith\n\n```ts\nbuild: {\n  sourcemap: mode === \"production\" && !!process.env.SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN ? \"hidden\" : false,\n  ...\n}\n```\n\n`\"hidden\"` is preferable to `true` because it also strips the `//# sourceMappingURL=` comment from the emitted JS (so curious visitors can't discover the `.map` URL from the deployed bundle), while still uploading symbolicated maps to Sentry. Alternatives: add a post-build `rm dist/**/*.map` step in CI that runs unconditionally, or move the deletion into a tiny separate Vite plugin that is also unconditional.", "corroborated_by": null, "source_bug_ids": ["bug_005"], "created_at": 1780028209.0389628, "_file_path": "/tmp/bughunter_work/reports/bug_005_report.json"}, {"bug_id": "bug_002", "name": "Sentry user_role tag not cleared on sign-out + stale across user switches", "severity": "nit", "file_path": "src/hooks/useAuth.tsx", "start_line": 12, "end_line": 26, "pr_comment": "The `syncSentryUser` helper in `src/hooks/useAuth.tsx` has two telemetry-correctness issues. (1) On sign-out, `Sentry.setTag('user_role', null)` does not delete the tag \u2014 Sentry's Scope.setTag stores the value as-is, so subsequent events carry a literal `user_role: null`, polluting dashboards and breaking tag-based filters. (2) When a new user signs in, there is a window between `user` changing and `fetchProfileAndRole` completing in which `role` is still `null`; the `if (role)` guard then skips the `setTag` call, leaving the previous user's role tag attached to any errors captured during that window \u2014 cross-user role mislabeling. Fix: skip the `setTag` call in the unauthenticated branch (or pass `undefined`), and either reset `role` state at the start of `signIn`/in the silent-update path or always overwrite the tag (e.g. with a sentinel like `'unknown'`) inside the effect.", "reasoning": "### What the bug is\n\n`syncSentryUser` (src/hooks/useAuth.tsx:12-26) runs via a `useEffect` keyed on `[user, role, profile?.full_name]` (line 69-71). It has two failure modes:\n\n**1. `setTag('user_role', null)` on sign-out does not remove the tag.**\n\nIn `@sentry/core`'s `Scope.setTag`, the implementation is literally `this._tags = { ...this._tags, [key]: value }` \u2014 the value is stored as-is. There is no `removeTag` API in the SDK. So passing `null` records a literal `null` in the tags map, which serializes into outgoing events as `tags: { user_role: null }`. Any error captured after sign-out (e.g., a network failure on the login screen, a render error during the auth-loading state) is sent to Sentry tagged `user_role: null` instead of having no `user_role` tag at all.\n\n**2. The previous user's role tag persists across user switches.**\n\nWhen user A signs out and user B signs in, the sequence on the auth side is: `onAuthStateChange` fires with the new session \u2192 `setUser(newSession.user)` \u2192 async `fetchProfileAndRole(userId)` starts \u2192 `setRole(primaryRole)` runs only after the Supabase queries return.\n\nThe effect on `[user, role, profile?.full_name]` fires the moment `user` changes, so `syncSentryUser(newUser, role=\u003cpreviousValue\u003e, fullName=\u2026)` is called with a stale `role`. In the silent-update path (line ~167, `isSilentUpdate = TOKEN_REFRESHED || (SIGNED_IN && isSameUser)`), or right after `signOut` followed by `signIn`, `role` state still holds the previous value (`signIn` at line ~243 only resets `fetchingForRef`, not `role` state). Inside `syncSentryUser`, the `if (role) Sentry.setTag('user_role', role)` branch is `true` with the old role \u2014 so events captured in that gap are mislabeled with the prior user's role.\n\n### Step-by-step proof\n\nConsider this concrete sequence:\n\n1. User A (role `student`) signs in. After `fetchProfileAndRole` completes, the effect fires: `Sentry.setUser({id: A})`; `Sentry.setTag('user_role', 'student')`. Sentry scope now has `user_role: 'student'`.\n2. User A calls `signOut()`. This sets `user=null, role=null` (line ~261-265). The effect fires with `user=null` and enters the unauthenticated branch: `Sentry.setUser(null)` + `Sentry.setTag('user_role', null)`. Sentry scope now has user cleared but `tags._user_role === null` \u2014 literally null, not absent.\n3. User encounters a network error while loading the login screen. Sentry captures the event. The outgoing payload includes `tags: { user_role: null }`. In the Sentry UI, this event is filed under `user_role: null`, breaking the dashboard query `tag:user_role:student` (it appears in neither set) and showing a literal-null bucket that operators must remember to ignore.\n4. User B (role `teacher`) signs in. `onAuthStateChange` fires SIGNED_IN; `setUser(B)` runs immediately; `fetchProfileAndRole(B.id)` starts asynchronously. Between these two events the effect fires with `user=B, role=null` (since the prior signOut reset it). `if (role)` is false, so `setTag` is skipped \u2014 leaving the previously-set `user_role: null` in place. If an error happens here (e.g., the prefetched dashboard route fails), it's tagged `user_role: null` though it belongs to user B.\n5. Variation: if instead user A logs out and B logs in *without* the intervening null-write (e.g., a TOKEN_REFRESHED to a different account, or signIn called while still holding A's state), the effect fires with the new `user=B` and stale `role='student'`. Events captured in the gap before `fetchProfileAndRole` finishes are tagged `user_role: student` while actually being user B \u2014 cross-user role attribution.\n\n### Why existing code doesn't prevent it\n\nThere is no `removeTag` API and no explicit `role` reset on sign-in. The effect dependency array on `[user, role, profile?.full_name]` correctly re-fires when each changes, but cannot synchronize `user` and `role` (they update independently because `role` requires a DB round-trip).\n\n### Impact\n\nTelemetry-quality bug, not a functional one. Filter expressions like `tag:user_role:student` miss events tagged `null`; dashboards split by `user_role` show a null bucket; brief windows of cross-user role mislabeling can mislead post-mortem analysis (\"a teacher hit this error\" when actually a student did, or vice versa). Severity is reasonably **nit** \u2014 no user-visible defect.\n\n### Fix\n\nTwo small changes in src/hooks/useAuth.tsx:\n\n```ts\nfunction syncSentryUser(user, role, fullName) {\n  if (!user) {\n    Sentry.setUser(null);\n    // Don't write null \u2014 Sentry has no removeTag. Let the next sign-in overwrite,\n    // or use a sentinel like 'unauthenticated' if you want the tag explicitly present.\n    return;\n  }\n  Sentry.setUser({ id: user.id, ...(fullName ? { username: fullName } : {}) });\n  // Always set, so the previous user's role doesn't linger during the role-fetch window.\n  Sentry.setTag('user_role', role ?? 'unknown');\n}\n```\n\nAnd in `signIn` (and on the silent-update path), reset `role` state to `null` immediately so the effect doesn't briefly fire with the previous user's role.\n\n### On the duplicate-refutation\n\nOne refutation flags this as a duplicate of bug_007 and asks for it to be dropped. Per the synthesis description, bug_007 was *merged into* this finding (bug_002 has the lower id and additionally documents the cross-user mislabeling window, which bug_007 did not). Multiple verifiers independently confirmed both halves of the finding on their merits. The merge is the correct outcome \u2014 there is one comment to file, and it is this one.", "corroborated_by": null, "source_bug_ids": ["bug_002"], "created_at": 1780028219.44669, "_file_path": "/tmp/bughunter_work/reports/bug_002_report.json"}, {"bug_id": "bug_001", "name": "denyUrls regex /extensions?\\// silently drops real errors", "severity": "nit", "file_path": "src/lib/sentry.ts", "start_line": 116, "end_line": 122, "pr_comment": "`/extensions?\\//i` in the new `denyUrls` (src/lib/sentry.ts:116) is unanchored, so it matches the substring `extension/` or `extensions/` anywhere in a script URL \u2014 not real browser-extension origins, which use `scheme://` (e.g. `chrome-extension://abc/foo.js`) and never get matched by this pattern. The three anchored entries immediately below (`/^chrome-extension:\\/\\//i`, `/^moz-extension:\\/\\//i`, `/^safari-extension:\\/\\//i`) already cover every real extension scheme, making the bare pattern simultaneously redundant for its apparent intent and unsafely broad \u2014 any future third-party script or asset path containing `/extension(s)/` would have its errors silently dropped. Suggest removing it or anchoring to a scheme like `/-extension:\\/\\//i`.", "reasoning": "**What the bug is.** Line 116 of `src/lib/sentry.ts` adds `/extensions?\\//i` as the first entry in `denyUrls`. The `?` makes the trailing `s` optional, so this matches `extension/` or `extensions/`. It is NOT anchored with `^`, so it matches the substring anywhere in the string. Sentry compares each `denyUrls` regex against the top stack-frame `abs_path` (the script URL, e.g. `https://app.example.com/assets/index-abc.js`).\n\n**Why this is wrong for the stated intent.** Real browser-extension origins are `chrome-extension://\u003cid\u003e/\u003cfile\u003e`, `moz-extension://\u003cid\u003e/\u003cfile\u003e`, `safari-extension://\u003cid\u003e/\u003cfile\u003e`. After the literal `extension` comes `:`, not `/`. So `/extensions?\\//i` **never matches a real extension URL** \u2014 the three explicit anchored patterns immediately below it (`/^chrome-extension:\\/\\//i`, `/^moz-extension:\\/\\//i`, `/^safari-extension:\\/\\//i`) are what actually catch those. The bare pattern adds zero extension-origin coverage that the anchored ones do not already provide.\n\n**Why it is also unsafely broad.** Any script URL whose path contains `/extension/` or `/extensions/` as a segment will match \u2014 examples: a third-party widget served from `https://cdn.vendor.com/static/extensions/widget.js`, or any future internal asset/route bundled under a path containing the substring (e.g. an admin page that lazy-loads `/assets/extensions-page-abc.js` if a route or chunk name were named that way). Errors originating in such files would be silently dropped before reaching Sentry.\n\n**Addressing the refutation.** The refuter is correct that today's `vite.config.ts` `manualChunks` (lines 93-108) produces chunk names like `vendor-react`, `vendor-supabase`, `vendor-radix`, etc. \u2014 none contain `extension`, so the current bundle output does not collide with this regex. That is why we are filing this as a **nit** rather than `normal`: there is no smoking-gun present-tense data loss in the build as it stands. But two things keep this worth flagging: (1) the pattern is dead code for its apparent intent \u2014 it cannot match any real extension origin, so leaving it in misleads readers into thinking extension filtering is broader than it is; (2) it is a hidden footgun for the future \u2014 the moment any chunk, lazy-loaded route, or third-party script lives under a path containing `/extension(s)/`, errors from it disappear silently without any indication that this regex is the cause.\n\n**Step-by-step proof.** Given the regex `r = /extensions?\\//i`:\n  1. `r.test(\"chrome-extension://abcdef/script.js\")` \u2192 `false` (after `extension` comes `:`, not `/`)\n  2. `r.test(\"moz-extension://xyz/content.js\")` \u2192 `false`\n  3. `r.test(\"https://cdn.example.com/static/extensions/widget.js\")` \u2192 `true` (matches `extensions/`)\n  4. `r.test(\"https://app.example.com/assets/admin-extension-page-abc.js\")` \u2192 `false` (no `/` immediately after `extension`)\n  5. `r.test(\"https://app.example.com/assets/admin/extensions/page-abc.js\")` \u2192 `true`\n\nCases (1) and (2) show the pattern fails its apparent purpose. Cases (3) and (5) show it would silently swallow real errors if any such URL ever appears. The explicit `/^chrome-extension:\\/\\//i` etc. on lines 118-120 already do (1) and (2) correctly.\n\n**Fix.** Drop the bare pattern entirely \u2014 the three anchored scheme patterns already cover every real browser-extension origin. If you specifically want to keep a scheme-agnostic extension filter, anchor it to a scheme: `/-extension:\\/\\//i` matches `chrome-extension://`, `moz-extension://`, `safari-extension://`, plus any future `*-extension://` scheme, with no risk of matching ordinary URL paths.", "corroborated_by": null, "source_bug_ids": ["bug_001"], "created_at": 1780028213.3552916, "_file_path": "/tmp/bughunter_work/reports/bug_001_report.json"}, {"bug_id": "bug_010", "name": "Session Replay records essay images despite essay/redacao redaction intent", "severity": "normal", "file_path": "src/lib/sentry.ts", "start_line": 91, "end_line": 98, "pr_comment": "Session Replay is enabled with `replaysOnErrorSampleRate: 1.0` in production and the integration uses `blockAllMedia: false` (src/lib/sentry.ts:96), but the file's own `SENSITIVE_KEYWORDS` list explicitly redacts `essay`/`redacao`/`reda\u00e7\u00e3o` from error events \u2014 a clear signal that essay content is considered PII here. Because `\u003cimg\u003e` elements are recorded in the replay DOM mutations and the teacher correction view (src/components/teacher/EssayCorrection.tsx:122) renders student handwritten essays via publicly-fetchable Supabase URLs (`\u003cimg src={selectedEssay.imageUrl}\u003e`), an errored teacher session leaks the source URL into the replay, and anyone with Sentry access can re-fetch the original handwritten essay (potentially including the student's name on paper). The `beforeSend` keyword sanitizer does not run on replay events, so the existing redaction does not cover this path. Fix: set `blockAllMedia: true` in the replay integration, or add `.sentry-block`/`[data-private]` to the essay `\u003cimg\u003e` elements in EssayWriterView and EssayCorrection.", "reasoning": "## What the bug is\n\nThis PR enables Sentry Session Replay in production for the first time (replaysOnErrorSampleRate: 1.0, replaysSessionSampleRate: 0.05), wired to `Sentry.replayIntegration({ maskAllText: true, maskAllInputs: true, blockAllMedia: false, ... })` in src/lib/sentry.ts:91-98. The masking config shows the team carefully thought about protecting text and form inputs \u2014 but `blockAllMedia: false` opts out of the equivalent protection for `\u003cimg\u003e`/`\u003cvideo\u003e`/`\u003caudio\u003e`/`\u003cpicture\u003e`/`\u003ciframe\u003e`/`\u003cembed\u003e`. Per the Sentry docs, when `blockAllMedia` is false the replay records the element's `src` attribute (it does not just record a placeholder), and the replay viewer renders the live URL at playback time.\n\n## Why this conflicts with documented intent\n\nsrc/lib/sentry.ts:6-19 defines `SENSITIVE_KEYWORDS` which explicitly includes `essay`, `redacao`, and `reda\u00e7\u00e3o`. The `beforeSend` hook walks the error event and replaces values under matching keys with `[REDACTED]`. That is unambiguous evidence the team treats essay content as PII \u2014 but `beforeSend` only fires for error events. Sentry Session Replay ships through a separate transport and has its own `beforeSendReplay` hook, which is not configured here. So the existing redaction does not extend to replay snapshots at all.\n\n## Concrete leak path (step-by-step)\n\n1. A student uploads a handwritten-essay photo via `\u003cinput type=\"file\" accept=\"image/*\"\u003e` in src/components/student/EssayWriterView.tsx:181. The file is uploaded to `supabase.storage.from('essays')` and its public URL is saved.\n2. A teacher opens src/components/teacher/EssayCorrection.tsx. At line 122 the component renders `\u003cimg src={selectedEssay.imageUrl} ... /\u003e`, where `imageUrl` is the persistent Supabase public URL.\n3. The teacher hits any unhandled error during correction (network blip, render bug, an error from a downstream API call, etc.). `replaysOnErrorSampleRate: 1.0` guarantees the session's replay buffer is uploaded to Sentry.\n4. `blockAllMedia: false` and the absence of `.sentry-block`/`[data-private]` on the essay `\u003cimg\u003e` element mean the `src` URL is recorded in the DOM mutation stream as-is.\n5. Anyone with Sentry project access plays back the replay; the replay viewer fetches the public Supabase URL and renders the handwritten essay \u2014 which may include the student's name on paper, draft answers, school info, etc.\n\n## Why existing safeguards don't catch it\n\n- `maskAllText: true` only affects text nodes, not `\u003cimg\u003e` `src` attributes.\n- `maskAllInputs: true` only masks form inputs, not preview/render images.\n- `block: ['[data-private]', '.sentry-block']` would catch the image, but neither EssayWriterView.tsx nor EssayCorrection.tsx attaches those markers to the essay `\u003cimg\u003e` elements.\n- `beforeSend` (and therefore the `SENSITIVE_KEYWORDS` redaction) does not run on replay events.\n\n## On the EssayWriterView blob URL nuance\n\nOne verifier noted that the student-side preview uses `URL.createObjectURL(file)` (EssayWriterView.tsx:162), producing a `blob:` URL that is session-local and not dereferenceable from the Sentry replay viewer. That partially mitigates the writer-side preview, but it does not mitigate the teacher-side flow \u2014 EssayCorrection.tsx:122 uses persistent Supabase public URLs, which are fetchable by anyone who can read the replay DOM. The bug stands on the teacher-correction path even if the student-side preview only leaks a useless blob URL.\n\n## Impact\n\nPII leak: handwritten student essays (potentially including the student's name written on the page, school info, draft personal content) become accessible to anyone with Sentry project access whenever a teacher's correction session errors. This is the exact content type the codebase already treats as sensitive elsewhere in the same file.\n\n## Fix\n\nSimplest: change `blockAllMedia: false` to `blockAllMedia: true` in src/lib/sentry.ts:96. This is Sentry's documented default and aligns replay privacy with the existing text/input masking. Alternative (more surgical): add `className=\"sentry-block\"` or `data-private` to the essay `\u003cimg\u003e` elements in src/components/teacher/EssayCorrection.tsx:122 and src/components/student/EssayWriterView.tsx:192. For defense in depth, also add a `beforeSendReplay` hook that strips known-sensitive URL patterns.", "corroborated_by": null, "source_bug_ids": ["bug_010"], "created_at": 1780028220.250755, "_file_path": "/tmp/bughunter_work/reports/bug_010_report.json"}]
> 01:17
